description:
You are a network security analyst performing a TLS configuration audit on captured HTTPS egress traffic. The file ~/Documents/captures/tls_traffic.pcap is already loaded in Wireshark. Analyze all TLS handshakes in the capture and produce a structured security audit report saved to ~/Documents/tls_audit_report.txt using exactly the following format:

TLS SECURITY AUDIT REPORT
==========================
Total Client Hello messages: <number>
Total completed handshakes (Server Hello): <number>
Unique SNI hostnames: <comma-separated list>
TLS versions in Client Hello (record layer): <comma-separated list, e.g. TLS 1.0, TLS 1.2>
Weak TLS versions detected: <Yes or No> (TLS 1.0 or 1.1 are considered weak)
Server-selected cipher suites: <comma-separated list of hex codes or standard names>

All values must reflect the actual capture contents.
